_meta.description: Canned fallback responses for the feedback widget when the /api/draft-feedback endpoint returns {fallback: 'canned'}. The client widget iterates patterns top-to-bottom and uses the first match. 'generic' is the final catch-all.

responses.response: routing to vishal's queue — copy feedback. the line you're pointing at is doing too much; i'll cut it in half and see if the next sentence still lands. if it does, the original was load-bearing on a vibe, not a thought.
vishal's queue. voice drift is the failure mode i'm most worried about — if it reads like a landing page, the doc has stopped doing its job. concrete fix: strip the adjective and keep the noun.

akhil's queue — scope question, not a copy fix. either the doc needs to name the gap explicitly (so the reader stops looking) or the scenario needs to include it. i think it's the former; the demo is a slice, not a survey.

akhil's queue — this is the kind of disagreement the review loop is built for. take it as a comment on the doc, not the demo; if the argument doesn't survive the push-back, the rewrite should be visible inside the scenario itself.
vishal's queue. fact-level or surface-level errors get fixed in place, not debated. flagging it.
noted. the parts that work are the parts to protect during the next round of changes — i'll mark it so akhil doesn't quietly rewrite it on the review pass.
akhil's queue. 'more' usually means the doc didn't pay off a promise it made earlier; the fix is either to pay it off or to not make the promise. i lean toward not making the promise.
vishal's queue. length is a copy problem until proven otherwise. i'll do a cut pass and see what falls out without anyone noticing.

both queues. clarity failures are usually a structure problem dressed as a copy problem — akhil takes the structure, vishal takes the surface. if the structure is fine, the copy fix is small. if it isn't, the copy fix is wasted work.
demo-layer issue, not doc-layer. the scenario shouldn't be doing animation work that distracts from the writing surface; if the motion is what you remember, the motion is too loud.
meta feedback on the scenario itself. the scenario is supposed to show one thing clearly; if it's showing two, one of them has to leave. naming which one is the useful next move.

logged. without an api key the architect can only draft a stub; the real reply would route this to vishal or akhil depending on whether it's a surface fix or a structural one. for now: assume both queues see it.
